channel.title: Orthodox Christian Sermons: 22nd Sunday after Pentecost
channel.description: Audio Orthodox Christian Sermons: 22nd Sunday after Pentecost

item.title: 22nd Sunday after Pentecost, Parable Of The Rich Man And Lazarus Summary, Concerning Parables And True Salvation, 2010-11-07
item.description: :22nd Sunday after Pentecost. A short summary of the main points of the parable of the Rich man and Lazarus. This was part of a short after liturgy talk, because the parable was not the subject of the homily after the Gospel at liturgy.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :22nd Sunday after Pentecost. A short summary of the main points of the parable of the Rich man and Lazarus. This was part of a short after liturgy talk, because the parable was not the subject of the homily after the Gospel at liturgy.

item.title: 22nd Sunday after Pentecost, God Forbid That I Should Glory Save In The Cross Of Our Lord Jesus Christ By Whom The World Is Crucified Unto Me And I Unto The World, 2011-11-13
item.description: :22nd Sunday after Pentecost. God Forbid That I Should Glory Save In The Cross Of Our Lord Jesus Christ By Whom The World Is Crucified Unto Me And I Unto The World, 2011-11-13
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :22nd Sunday after Pentecost. God Forbid That I Should Glory Save In The Cross Of Our Lord Jesus Christ By Whom The World Is Crucified Unto Me And I Unto The World, 2011-11-13

item.title: 22nd Sunday after Pentecost, 2012-11-04
item.description: :22nd Sunday after Pentecost. The Parable of the Rich Man and Lazarus is very complicated and teaches many things. We focus on two: everything in life is a trade; what do you want the most, pleasure now or happiness later? How do we remind ourselves of this trading, and trade well? Read the Scriptures.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :22nd Sunday after Pentecost. The Parable of the Rich Man and Lazarus is very complicated and teaches many things. We focus on two: everything in life is a trade; what do you want the most, pleasure now or happiness later? How do we remind ourselves of this trading, and trade well? Read the Scriptures.
